title: Reading Window Rail
prompt:
Create a production-ready Reading Window Rail using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original embedded reading effect models a document through three local checkpoints: Frame, Inspect, and Act. A reader selects a checkpoint, the orange window slides along a vertical rail, and a short passage updates beside it. This component must simulate reading progress inside its own card rather than listen to page scroll, ensuring it works in compact cards and full detail previews. Use a light Bento system with #F4F5F6 stage, white card, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, soft neutral shadow, #111111 type, and #4A4A4F secondary copy. Orange is the only bright accent, and the panel must use solid fills without gradients.

Use a labelled group of native 44px buttons with aria-pressed and an aria-live polite sentence. Click, Enter, and Space select a checkpoint. Move the rail window with a spring using stiffness 315 and damping 26; fade the passage over 150ms ease-out. Use local deterministic state only: no scroll listeners, timers, random values, storage, remote assets, network requests, browser globals, or layout measurement. Use transform and opacity for all animated effects.

At 320px, stack the passage and controls cleanly while retaining labels; at a large preview, preserve a comfortable reading line length. Include visible 3px focus rings, logical tab order, AA contrast, and written checkpoint feedback beyond orange. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by moving the window immediately with a brief opacity fade. Return complete client code, pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML: <section class="reading-window"><button aria-pressed="true">1. Frame</button></section> */
.reading-window{max-width:340px;padding:20px;border-left:8px solid #f4f5f6;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.reading-window button{display:block;min-height:44px;width:100%;margin:6px 0;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:12px;background:#fff;color:#111;font-weight:800;text-align:left}.reading-window button[aria-pressed=true]{border-left:5px solid #ff9950}.reading-window button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.reading-window *{transition:none}}

demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [a,setA]=React.useState(0);const s=[['Frame','Set the reader’s starting context.'],['Inspect','Pause on the clearest evidence.'],['Act','Record the next reversible action.']];return
READING WINDOW{s[a][0]}

}return ;})()
